---
title: "Uber Ride Data Analysis for Revenue Optimization"
author: "Alekhya Gangopadhyay"
output: word_document
---

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Load dataset
uber <- read.csv("D:/Downloads/ncr_ride_bookings.csv")

# Remove NA
uber <- na.omit(uber)

# -----------------------------
# FIX COLUMN NAMES (IMPORTANT)
# -----------------------------
names(uber) <- make.names(names(uber))

# -----------------------------
# COMBINE DATE + TIME
# -----------------------------
uber$datetime <- as.POSIXct(
  paste(uber$Date, uber$Time),
  format="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"
)

# Extract features
uber$hour <- factor(format(uber$datetime, "%H"))

uber$day <- factor(
  format(uber$datetime, "%A"),
  levels = c("Monday","Tuesday","Wednesday","Thursday","Friday","Saturday","Sunday")
)

# -----------------------------
# CLEAN REVENUE COLUMN
# -----------------------------
uber$Booking.Value <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.]", "", uber$Booking.Value))

# -----------------------------
# ANALYSIS
# -----------------------------

# Rides by hour
ggplot(uber, aes(x=hour)) +
  geom_bar(fill="blue") +
  ggtitle("Rides by Hour")

# Rides by day
ggplot(uber, aes(x=day)) +
  geom_bar(fill="green") +
  ggtitle("Rides by Day")

# Revenue analysis
hourly_rev <- uber %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarise(
    trips = n(),
    revenue = sum(Booking.Value, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

hourly_rev

# Revenue trend
ggplot(hourly_rev, aes(x=hour, y=revenue, group=1)) +
  geom_line(color="red") +
  geom_point() +
  ggtitle("Revenue by Hour")

# Payment method analysis
if("Payment.Method" %in% names(uber)){
  ggplot(uber, aes(x=Payment.Method)) +
    geom_bar(fill="purple") +
    ggtitle("Payment Methods")
}

# Booking status analysis
if("Booking.Status" %in% names(uber)){
  ggplot(uber, aes(x=Booking.Status)) +
    geom_bar(fill="orange") +
    ggtitle("Booking Status")
}
